children.heading: Use of grant in violation of this part
children.label: 1 Use of grant in violation of this part

children.heading: General penalty
children.label: A General penalty
children.text: If an audit conducted under chapter 75 of title 31 finds that an amount paid to a State under for a fiscal year has been used in violation of this part, the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year quarter by the amount so used.section 603 of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Enhanced penalty for intentional violations
children.label: B Enhanced penalty for intentional violations
children.text: If the State does not prove to the satisfaction of the Secretary that the State did not intend to use the amount in violation of this part, the Secretary shall further reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year quarter by an amount equal to 5 percent of the State family assistance grant.section 603(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Penalty for misuse of competitive welfare-to-work funds
children.label: C Penalty for misuse of competitive welfare-to-work funds
children.text: If the Secretary of Labor finds that an amount paid to an entity under has been used in violation of subparagraph (B) or (C) of , the entity shall remit to the Secretary of Labor an amount equal to the amount so used.section 603(a)(5)(B) of this titlesection 603(a)(5) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a State has not, within 45 days after the end of a fiscal quarter, submitted the report required by for the quarter, the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year by an amount equal to 4 percent of the State family assistance grant.section 611(a) of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Rescission of penalty
children.label: ii Rescission of penalty
children.text: The Secretary shall rescind a penalty imposed on a State under clause (i) with respect to a report if the State submits the report before the end of the fiscal quarter that immediately succeeds the fiscal quarter for which the report was required.

children.heading: Report on engagement in additional work activities and expenditures for other benefits and services
children.label: B Report on engagement in additional work activities and expenditures for other benefits and services

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a State has not submitted the report required by by , or the report required by by , the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year by an amount equal to not more than 4 percent of the State family assistance grant.section 611(c)(1)(A)(i) of this titlesection 611(c)(1)(A)(ii) of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this titleMay 31, 2011August 31, 2011

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of the report required under , ; andsection 611(c)(1)(A)(i) of this titleJune 15, 2011

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of the report required under , .section 611(c)(1)(A)(ii) of this titleSeptember 15, 2011

children.heading: Penalty based on severity of failure
children.label: iii Penalty based on severity of failure
children.text: The Secretary shall impose a reduction under clause (i) with respect to a fiscal year based on the degree of noncompliance.

children.heading: Failure to satisfy minimum participation rates
children.label: 3 Failure to satisfy minimum participation rates

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a State to which a grant is made under for a fiscal year has failed to comply with for the fiscal year, the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year by an amount equal to the applicable percentage of the State family assistance grant.section 603 of this titlesection 607(a) of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: if a penalty was not imposed on the State under subparagraph (A) for the immediately preceding fiscal year, 5 percent; or

children.label: I
children.text: the percentage by which the grant payable to the State under was reduced for such preceding fiscal year, increased by 2 percentage points; orsection 603(a)(1) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: 21 percent.

children.heading: Penalty based on severity of failure
children.label: C Penalty based on severity of failure
children.text: The Secretary shall impose reductions under subparagraph (A) with respect to a fiscal year based on the degree of noncompliance, and may reduce the penalty if the noncompliance is due to circumstances that caused the State to become a needy State (as defined in ) during the fiscal year or if the noncompliance is due to extraordinary circumstances such as a natural disaster or regional recession. The Secretary shall provide a written report to Congress to justify any waiver or penalty reduction due to such extraordinary circumstances.section 603(b)(5) of this title

children.heading: Failure to participate in the income and eligibility verification system
children.label: 4 Failure to participate in the income and eligibility verification system
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a State program funded under this part is not participating during a fiscal year in the income and eligibility verification system required by , the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year by an amount equal to not more than 2 percent of the State family assistance grant.section 1320b–7 of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Failure to comply with paternity establishment and child support enforcement requirements under part D
children.label: 5 Failure to comply with paternity establishment and child support enforcement requirements under part D
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, if the Secretary determines that the State agency that administers a program funded under this part does not enforce the penalties requested by the agency administering part D against recipients of assistance under the State program who fail to cooperate in establishing paternity or in establishing, modifying, or enforcing a child support order in accordance with such part and who do not qualify for any good cause or other exception established by the State under , the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year (without regard to this section) by not more than 5 percent.section 654(29) of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Failure to timely repay a Federal Loan Fund for State Welfare Programs
children.label: 6 Failure to timely repay a Federal Loan Fund for State Welfare Programs
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a State has failed to repay any amount borrowed from the Federal Loan Fund for State Welfare Programs established under within the period of maturity applicable to the loan, plus any interest owed on the loan, the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year quarter (without regard to this section) by the outstanding loan amount, plus the interest owed on the outstanding amount. The Secretary shall not forgive any outstanding loan amount or interest owed on the outstanding amount.section 606 of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Failure of any State to maintain certain level of historic effort
children.label: 7 Failure of any State to maintain certain level of historic effort

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for a fiscal year by the amount (if any) by which qualified State expenditures for the then immediately preceding fiscal year are less than the applicable percentage of historic State expenditures with respect to such preceding fiscal year.section 603(a)(1) of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: Cash assistance, including any amount collected by the State as support pursuant to a plan approved under part D, on behalf of a family receiving assistance under the State program funded under this part, that is distributed to the family under and disregarded in determining the eligibility of the family for, and the amount of, such assistance.section 657(a)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: bb
children.text: Child care assistance.

children.label: cc
children.text: Educational activities designed to increase self-sufficiency, job training, and work, excluding any expenditure for public education in the State except expenditures which involve the provision of services or assistance to a member of an eligible family which is not generally available to persons who are not members of an eligible family.

children.label: dd
children.text: Administrative costs in connection with the matters described in items (aa), (bb), (cc), and (ee), but only to the extent that such costs do not exceed 15 percent of the total amount of qualified State expenditures for the fiscal year.

children.label: ee
children.text: Any other use of funds allowable under .section 604(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Exclusion of transfers from other State and local programs
children.label: II Exclusion of transfers from other State and local programs
aa
children.text: the expenditures exceed the amount expended under the State or local program in the fiscal year most recently ending before ; orAugust 22, 1996

children.label: bb
children.text: the State is entitled to a payment under former (as in effect immediately before ) with respect to the expenditures.section 603 of this titleAugust 22, 1996

children.heading: Exclusion of amounts expended to replace penalty grant reductions
children.label: III Exclusion of amounts expended to replace penalty grant reductions
children.text: Such term does not include any amount expended in order to comply with paragraph (12).

children.heading: Eligible families
children.label: IV Eligible families
children.text: As used in subclause (I), the term “eligible families” means families eligible for assistance under the State program funded under this part, families that would be eligible for such assistance but for the application of , and families of aliens lawfully present in the United States that would be eligible for such assistance but for the application of title IV of the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996 [ et seq.].section 608(a)(7) of this title8 U.S.C. 1601

children.heading: Counting of spending on certain pro-family activities
children.label: V Counting of spending on certain pro-family activities
children.text: The term “qualified State expenditures” includes the total expenditures by the State during the fiscal year under all State programs for a purpose described in paragraph (3) or (4) of .section 601(a) of this title

children.heading: Applicable percentage
children.label: ii Applicable percentage
children.text: The term “applicable percentage” means 80 percent (or, if the State meets the requirements of , 75 percent).section 607(a) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the expenditures by the State under parts A and F (as in effect during fiscal year 1994) for fiscal year 1994; or

children.label: aa
children.text: the State family assistance grant, plus the total amount required to be paid to the State under former for fiscal year 1994 with respect to amounts expended by the State for child care under subsection (g) or (i) of (as in effect during fiscal year 1994); bears tosection 603 of this titlesection 602 of this title

children.label: bb
children.text: the total amount required to be paid to the State under former (as in effect during fiscal year 1994) for fiscal year 1994.section 603 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: any expenditure from amounts made available by the Federal Government;

children.label: II
children.text: any State funds expended for the medicaid program under subchapter XIX;

children.label: III
children.text: any State funds which are used to match Federal funds provided under ; orsection 603(a)(5) of this title

children.label: IV
children.text: any State funds which are expended as a condition of receiving Federal funds other than under this part.

children.heading: Source of data
children.label: v Source of data
children.text: In determining expenditures by a State for fiscal years 1994 and 1995, the Secretary shall use information which was reported by the State on ACF Form 231 or (in the case of expenditures under part F) ACF Form 331, available as of the dates specified in clauses (ii) and (iii) of section 603(a)(1)(D) of this title.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Noncompliance of State child support enforcement program with requirements of part D
children.label: 8 Noncompliance of State child support enforcement program with requirements of part D
I
children.text: on the basis of data submitted by a State pursuant to , or on the basis of the results of a review conducted under , that the State program failed to achieve the paternity establishment percentages (as defined in ), or to meet other performance measures that may be established by the Secretary;section 654(15)(B) of this titlesection 652(a)(4) of this titlesection 652(g)(2) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: on the basis of the results of an audit or audits conducted under that the State data submitted pursuant to is incomplete or unreliable; orsection 652(a)(4)(C)(i) of this titlesection 654(15)(B) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: on the basis of the results of an audit or audits conducted under that a State failed to substantially comply with 1 or more of the requirements of part D (other than paragraph (24), or subparagraph (A) or (B)(i) of paragraph (27), of ); andsection 652(a)(4)(C) of this titlesection 654 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the State failed to take sufficient corrective action to achieve the appropriate performance levels or compliance as described in subparagraph (A)(i); or

children.label: II
children.text: the data submitted by the State pursuant to is incomplete or unreliable;section 654(15)(B) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: not less than 1 nor more than 2 percent;

children.label: ii
children.text: not less than 2 nor more than 3 percent, if the finding is the 2nd consecutive finding made pursuant to subparagraph (A); or

children.label: iii
children.text: not less than 3 nor more than 5 percent, if the finding is the 3rd or a subsequent consecutive such finding.

children.heading: Disregard of noncompliance which is of a technical nature
children.label: C Disregard of noncompliance which is of a technical nature
i
children.text: to have failed to have substantially complied with 1 or more of the requirements of part D shall be determined to have achieved substantial compliance only if the Secretary determines that the extent of the noncompliance is of a technical nature which does not adversely affect the performance of the State’s program under part D; or

children.label: ii
children.text: to have submitted incomplete or unreliable data pursuant to shall be determined to have submitted adequate data only if the Secretary determines that the extent of the incompleteness or unreliability of the data is of a technical nature which does not adversely affect the determination of the level of the State’s paternity establishment percentages (as defined under ) or other performance measures that may be established by the Secretary.section 654(15)(B) of this titlesection 652(g)(2) of this title

children.heading: Failure to comply with 5-year limit on assistance
children.label: 9 Failure to comply with 5-year limit on assistance
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a State has not complied with during a fiscal year, the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year by an amount equal to 5 percent of the State family assistance grant.section 608(a)(7) of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Failure of State receiving amounts from Contingency Fund to maintain 100 percent of historic effort
children.label: 10 Failure of State receiving amounts from Contingency Fund to maintain 100 percent of historic effort
children.text: If, at the end of any fiscal year during which amounts from the Contingency Fund for State Welfare Programs have been paid to a State, the Secretary finds that the qualified State expenditures (as defined in paragraph (7)(B)(i) (other than the expenditures described in subclause (I)(bb) of that paragraph)) under the State program funded under this part for the fiscal year are less than 100 percent of historic State expenditures (as defined in paragraph (7)(B)(iii) of this subsection), excluding any amount expended by the State for child care under subsection (g) or (i) of (as in effect during fiscal year 1994) for fiscal year 1994, the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year by the total of the amounts so paid to the State that the State has not remitted under .section 602 of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this titlesection 603(b)(6) of this title

children.heading: Failure to maintain assistance to adult single custodial parent who cannot obtain child care for child under age 6
children.label: 11 Failure to maintain assistance to adult single custodial parent who cannot obtain child care for child under age 6

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a State to which a grant is made under for a fiscal year has violated during the fiscal year, the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year by an amount equal to not more than 5 percent of the State family assistance grant.section 603 of this titlesection 607(e)(2) of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Penalty based on severity of failure
children.label: B Penalty based on severity of failure
children.text: The Secretary shall impose reductions under subparagraph (A) with respect to a fiscal year based on the degree of noncompliance.

children.heading: Requirement to expend additional State funds to replace grant reductions; penalty for failure to do so
children.label: 12 Requirement to expend additional State funds to replace grant reductions; penalty for failure to do so
A
children.text: not more than 2 percent of the State family assistance grant; and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of the expenditure required by the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Penalty for failure of State to maintain historic effort during year in which welfare-to-work grant is received
children.label: 13 Penalty for failure of State to maintain historic effort during year in which welfare-to-work grant is received
children.text: If a grant is made to a State under for a fiscal year and paragraph (7) of this subsection requires the grant payable to the State under to be reduced for the immediately succeeding fiscal year, then the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for such succeeding fiscal year by the amount of the grant made to the State under for the fiscal year.section 603(a)(5)(A) of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this titlesection 603(a)(5)(A) of this title

children.heading: Penalty for failure to reduce assistance for recipients refusing without good cause to work
children.label: 14 Penalty for failure to reduce assistance for recipients refusing without good cause to work

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a State to which a grant is made under in a fiscal year has violated during the fiscal year, the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year by an amount equal to not less than 1 percent and not more than 5 percent of the State family assistance grant.section 603 of this titlesection 607(e) of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Penalty based on severity of failure
children.label: B Penalty based on severity of failure
children.text: The Secretary shall impose reductions under subparagraph (A) with respect to a fiscal year based on the degree of noncompliance.

children.heading: Penalty for failure to establish or comply with work participation verification procedures
children.label: 15 Penalty for failure to establish or comply with work participation verification procedures

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a State to which a grant is made under in a fiscal year has violated during the fiscal year, the Secretary shall reduce the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year by an amount equal to not less than 1 percent and not more than 5 percent of the State family assistance grant.section 603 of this titlesection 607(i)(2) of this titlesection 603(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Penalty based on severity of failure
children.label: B Penalty based on severity of failure
children.text: The Secretary shall impose reductions under subparagraph (A) with respect to a fiscal year based on the degree of noncompliance.

children.heading: Penalty for failure to enforce spending policies
children.label: 16 Penalty for failure to enforce spending policies
i
children.text: the fiscal year immediately succeeding the year in which such 2-year period ends; and

children.label: ii
children.text: each succeeding fiscal year in which the State does not demonstrate that such State has implemented and maintained such policies and practices.

children.heading: Reduction of applicable penalty
children.label: B Reduction of applicable penalty
children.text: The Secretary may reduce the amount of the reduction required under subparagraph (A) based on the degree of noncompliance of the State.

children.heading: State not responsible for individual violations
children.label: C State not responsible for individual violations
children.text: Fraudulent activity by any individual in an attempt to circumvent the policies and practices required by shall not trigger a State penalty under subparagraph (A).section 608(a)(12) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may not impose a penalty on a State under subsection (a) with respect to a requirement if the Secretary determines that the State has reasonable cause for failing to comply with the requirement.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: Paragraph (1) of this subsection shall not apply to any penalty under paragraph (6), (7), (8), (10), (12), or (13) of subsection (a) and, with respect to the penalty under paragraph (2)(B) of subsection (a), shall only apply to the extent the Secretary determines that the reasonable cause for failure to comply with a requirement of that paragraph is as a result of a one-time, unexpected event, such as a widespread data system failure or a natural or man-made disaster.

children.heading: Notification of violation
children.label: A Notification of violation
children.text: Before imposing a penalty against a State under subsection (a) with respect to a violation of this part, the Secretary shall notify the State of the violation and allow the State the opportunity to enter into a corrective compliance plan in accordance with this subsection which outlines how the State will correct or discontinue, as appropriate, the violation and how the State will insure continuing compliance with this part.

children.heading: 60-day period to propose a corrective compliance plan
children.label: B 60-day period to propose a corrective compliance plan
children.text: During the 60-day period that begins on the date the State receives a notice provided under subparagraph (A) with respect to a violation, the State may submit to the Federal Government a corrective compliance plan to correct or discontinue, as appropriate, the violation.

children.heading: Consultation about modifications
children.label: C Consultation about modifications
children.text: During the 60-day period that begins with the date the Secretary receives a corrective compliance plan submitted by a State in accordance with subparagraph (B), the Secretary may consult with the State on modifications to the plan.

children.heading: Acceptance of plan
children.label: D Acceptance of plan
children.text: A corrective compliance plan submitted by a State in accordance with subparagraph (B) is deemed to be accepted by the Secretary if the Secretary does not accept or reject the plan during 60-day period that begins on the date the plan is submitted.

children.heading: Effect of correcting or discontinuing violation
children.label: 2 Effect of correcting or discontinuing violation
children.text: The Secretary may not impose any penalty under subsection (a) with respect to any violation covered by a State corrective compliance plan accepted by the Secretary if the State corrects or discontinues, as appropriate, the violation pursuant to the plan.

children.heading: Effect of failing to correct or discontinue violation
children.label: 3 Effect of failing to correct or discontinue violation
children.text: The Secretary shall assess some or all of a penalty imposed on a State under subsection (a) with respect to a violation if the State does not, in a timely manner, correct or discontinue, as appropriate, the violation pursuant to a State corrective compliance plan accepted by the Secretary.

children.heading: Inapplicability to certain penalties
children.label: 4 Inapplicability to certain penalties
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to the imposition of a penalty against a State under paragraph (2)(B), (6), (7), (8), (10), (12), (13), or (16) of subsection (a).

children.heading: Limitation on amount of penalties
children.label: d Limitation on amount of penalties

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In imposing the penalties described in subsection (a), the Secretary shall not reduce any quarterly payment to a State by more than 25 percent.

children.heading: Carryforward of unrecovered penalties
children.label: 2 Carryforward of unrecovered penalties
children.text: To the extent that paragraph (1) of this subsection prevents the Secretary from recovering during a fiscal year the full amount of penalties imposed on a State under subsection (a) of this section for a prior fiscal year, the Secretary shall apply any remaining amount of such penalties to the grant payable to the State under for the immediately succeeding fiscal year.section 603(a)(1) of this title
